findings.evidence: Line 291 `change (ofPath (γ.toPath.initialSegmentFamily t)).toPath = …` has no comment, unlike the documented `change`s at 405/408/498/508/546.
findings.fix: Replace with `rw [initialSegmentFamily]; exact toPath_ofPath _` (matching line 286), or add a comment noting why the wrapper must be exposed.
findings.issue: Undocumented `change` unfolding the `initialSegmentFamily` wrapper; every other defeq `change` in this file carries an explanatory comment.

findings.evidence: Lines 531-541: `source'`/`target'` proofs `change (γ.toPath.trans (… .cast _ _)) s = …` with no comment, unfolding the `append`/`ofPath` wrappers.
findings.fix: Document why the goal cannot be reached via `toPath_append`/`append` rewrite, or rewrite through an explicit lemma instead of the bare `change`.
findings.issue: Undocumented `change` relying on `append = ofPath (γ.toPath.trans …)` and `Path.cast` coercion defeq; same at line 541. This is the cross-wrapper/coercion defeq the rubric asks to flag.

summary: Proofs are robust and well-factored, but three `change` tactics that bridge wrapper/coercion definitional equality lack the documenting comment the author applies at every other such site. Add a one-line justification or use an explicit lemma.
